question: [File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML file with deeply nested anchor/alias structures (YAML bomb) via the CONFIG_PATH environment variable, causing yaml.load() to exhaust process memory and crash the mirror node REST service, making it unavailable to all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user write a YAML file with thousands of deeply nested objects to CONFIG_PATH, causing extend(true, config, doc) to trigger a call-stack overflow via recursive deep-merge, crashing the Node.js process on startup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user place a multi-gigabyte YAML file at CONFIG_PATH, causing fs.readFileSync(configFile, 'utf8') to load the entire file into memory synchronously, exhausting heap space and crashing the REST service before it serves any request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a YAML file with a deeply recursive merge-key (<<) chain at CONFIG_PATH, causing yaml.load() to enter exponential expansion and exhaust CPU/memory, resulting in a non-network DoS of the mirror node REST API?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML file containing a circular reference via anchors/aliases at CONFIG_PATH, causing extend(true, config, doc) to enter an infinite loop during deep-merge, permanently hanging the Node.js process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user set CONFIG_PATH to a directory containing both application.yml and application.yaml, causing loadYaml() to be called twice and extend() to double-merge a malicious payload, amplifying memory consumption and causing a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: load()] Can an unprivileged external user set CONFIG_PATH to a path pointing to a named pipe or special device file, causing fs.existsSync() and fs.readFileSync() to block indefinitely, hanging the startup of the REST service and causing a DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: loadEnvironment()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a very large number of environment variables prefixed with HIERO_MIRROR_ or HEDERA_MIRROR_, causing loadEnvironment() to iterate and call setConfigValue() thousands of times, exhausting CPU during startup and delaying or crashing the REST service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setConfigValue()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable with an extremely long name (e.g., HIERO_MIRROR_REST_ followed by thousands of underscores), causing propertyPath.toLowerCase().split('_') to produce a massive array and the nested for-loop in setConfigValue() to perform O(n*m) iterations, exhausting CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setConfigValue()] Can an unprivileged external user inject environment variables that match the HIERO_MIRROR prefix but have thousands of underscore-separated segments, causing the inner Object.entries(current) loop in setConfigValue() to traverse the entire config tree repeatedly, causing O(n²) CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setConfigValue()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_REST_DB_POOL_MAXCONNECTIONS with value '0' or '-1', bypassing parseDbPoolConfig() validation and setting maxConnections to a value that causes the DB pool to reject all connections, resulting in a complete DoS of all API endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: setConfigValue()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_REST_DB_POOL_STATEMENTTIMEOUT with value '0', causing parseDbPoolConfig() to throw InvalidConfigError and crash the REST service on startup, resulting in a persistent DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: convertType()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable with value 'Infinity', causing convertType() to return the JavaScript Infinity value via +value, which when assigned to db.pool.maxConnections bypasses the parsed <= 0 check and sets an unbounded pool size, causing resource exhaustion DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: convertType()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable with a very long numeric string (e.g., 10000 digits), causing isNaN(value) and +value to perform expensive string-to-number conversion, and when done for many env vars simultaneously, causing CPU exhaustion during startup?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: convertType()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an environment variable with value '-0', causing convertType() to return -0 via +value, which passes the isNaN check but may cause unexpected behavior in parseDbPoolConfig() where parsed <= 0 evaluates to true for -0, throwing InvalidConfigError and crashing the service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseDbPoolConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_DB_POOL_CONNECTIONTIMEOUT=1 to set an extremely short connection timeout, causing all DB pool connections to time out immediately under any load, resulting in a non-network DoS where all API requests fail with connection timeout errors?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseDbPoolConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_DB_POOL_MAXCONNECTIONS=1 to reduce the connection pool to a single connection, causing all concurrent API requests to queue and time out, effectively creating a non-network DoS under normal traffic load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseDbPoolConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_DB_POOL_STATEMENTTIMEOUT=1 to set a 1ms statement timeout, causing every database query to be killed before completion, resulting in all API endpoints returning errors and a complete non-network DoS of the mirror node REST service?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseDurationConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_QUERY_MAXTIMESTAMPRANGE=null, causing parseDuration(null) to return a falsy value, throwing InvalidConfigError and crashing the REST service on startup, resulting in a persistent DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseDurationConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_QUERY_MAXRECORDFILECLOSEINTERVAL with a crafted duration string that causes parseDuration() to return a very large millisecond value, and BigInt(ms) * NANOSECONDS_PER_MILLISECOND to produce an astronomically large BigInt, causing subsequent timestamp range comparisons to never enforce limits and allowing unbounded DB queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseQueryConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_QUERY_MAXTIMESTAMPRANGE with a very large duration (e.g., '999999d'), causing maxTimestampRangeNs to be set to an enormous BigInt value, effectively disabling the timestamp range limit and allowing users to submit queries spanning years of data, exhausting DB resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseQueryConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_QUERY_MAXTRANSACTIONSTIMESTAMPRANGE with a very large duration string, causing the maxTransactionsTimestampRangeNs guard in bindTimestampRange() to be effectively disabled, allowing unbounded timestamp range queries that exhaust database CPU and memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: parseQueryConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user inject HIERO_MIRROR_REST_QUERY_MAXREPEATEDQUERYPARAMETERS with a very large integer value, disabling the repeated query parameter limit enforced by isRepeatedQueryParameterValidLength(), allowing users to send requests with thousands of repeated parameters that exhaust server memory and CPU?
